name: thieves-guild-hideout
summary: A thieves' guild den — mismatched furniture, a planning table with a city map, a fence's trading counter, loot stashed in chests. Multiple exits (door, trapdoor, shuttered window). Dimly lit, watchful.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle or irregular — hideouts aren't designed, they're occupied

primary_palette.note: planning-forward — central map table for jobs
fence-forward — trading counter where loot is appraised
mess-hall style — guild hangout, long table for meals and meetings

story_prompts: heist planning — map covered in chalk, members arguing over approaches
fence at work — stolen goods sorted into piles, ledger updated quietly
guild betrayal — one thief bound in a chair, others arguing about fate
raid — guards breaking down the door, thieves scrambling for the trapdoor

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low shadowy light (0.25-0.35) — always a reason to hide

anti_patterns: always at least two exits — trapdoor, back door, or secret passage implied
don't make it orderly — mismatched, patched, functional-over-beautiful
keep the fence's counter distinct from the planning table — different functions
